senses.definition: A family of biting flies in the order Diptera, commonly called blackflies.
examples: Researchers collected specimens of simuliidae from the riverbank.
The simuliidae are known for their painful bites.
Medical texts discuss diseases transmitted by simuliidae.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling the Simuliidae (black flies).
examples: The simulioid features were observed in the specimen's mouthparts.
An insect with simulioid traits was identified in the sample.

senses.definition: A simulioid insect or organism; a member of or resembling Simuliidae.
examples: The museum label described the specimen as a simulioid.
Researchers compared the simulioid to known black flies.

senses.definition: A genus of black flies (family Simuliidae); the type genus of the family.
examples: Simulium includes many species that are vectors of river blindness.
An adult Simulium was captured near the stream.

senses.definition: Plural form of Simulium; multiple black flies of the genus Simulium.
examples: The riverbank showed many simuliums resting on vegetation.
Experts warned about simuliums during the wet season.

senses.definition: To imitate or simulate something; to create a model or representation of real conditions.
examples: The software can simulize real-world traffic patterns.
Researchers simulize environmental changes on a computer model.

senses.definition: Short for simulations or simulators in some technical contexts; plural form of simul.
examples: The lab ran several sims; the simuls results matched expectations.
Engineers compared different simuls to choose the best model.

senses.definition: The quality of existing or occurring at the same time; concurrent occurrences.
examples: The simultaneities of events made the investigation interesting.
Artists explored simultaneities in their works.

senses.definition: The state of happening at the same time; concurrency.
examples: The simultaneity of the two events surprised the observers.
In physics, simultaneity is relative to the observer.

senses.definition: Happening at the same time; concurrent.
examples: The two concerts happened at the simultaneous time.
They spoke in simultaneous voices.

senses.definition: Relating to multiple things occurring at the same time; alternative plural form of simultaneous (historical/rare usage).
examples: In archaic texts, simulatoneous forms are described as simultaneouses.
The manuscript uses a rarely encountered simultaneouses construction.

senses.definition: At the same time; concurrently.
examples: The two lights flashed simultaneously.
She began to speak and laugh simultaneously.

senses.definition: The quality or condition of existing or occurring at the same time.
examples: The simultaneousness of events complicated the timeline.
Scholars noted the simultaneousness in the narrative.

senses.definition: Plural of simultaneousness; multiple instances of concurrency.
examples: Researchers discussed several simultaneousnesses in the dataset.
The report lists various simultaneousnesses across departments.

senses.definition: An obsolete or rare term; may refer to a state of being simultaneous in older texts.
examples: The term simulty appears in some archaic dictionaries.
Scholars note simulty as a historical variant.

senses.definition: A legendary bird (also known as Simorgh) in Persian mythology, often depicted as a wise, benevolent phoenix-like creature.
examples: The poet invoked the simurg as a symbol of wisdom.
In stories, the simurg soars above the desert.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of simurg; a legendary benevolent bird in Persian mythology.
examples: The simurgh is often described as a guide to heroes.
Ancient tales celebrate the simurgh's wisdom.

senses.definition: Plural of simurgh; multiple mythic birds in Persian lore.
examples: Ancient tales mention several simurghs in different legends.
The cliffs are said to be watched over by simurghs.

senses.definition: Plural of simurg; alternate plural form of the legendary bird Simorgh.
examples: In the epic, the simurgs appear as guardians of the forest.
The illustration shows a gathering of simurgs.

senses.definition: A prescription drug used to lower cholesterol, belonging to the statin class.
examples: She started taking simvastatin to reduce her cholesterol.
Simvastatin is often prescribed with diet changes.

senses.definition: An act that is morally wrong or against religious or societal rules.
examples: Lying is a sin in many religions.
He asked for forgiveness for his sin.

senses.definition: To commit a sin; to do something morally wrong.
examples: She sinned by breaking the promise.
People strive not to sin.

senses.definition: An Asian city or region name in historical or literary contexts; may also appear as a surname or acronym in some contexts.
examples: The caravan passed through Sina in ancient maps.
Sina is mentioned in several classical texts.

senses.definition: Historical plural form referring to the Chinese or Sinic peoples in classical texts.
examples: The travelers described the lands of the Sinae.
Medieval writers often used Sinae to refer to China.

senses.definition: Relating to Sinae (historical reference to China) or to a Sinaean style or culture.
examples: Sinaean artifacts were traded along the Silk Road.
The book discusses Sinaean influence on early navigation.

senses.definition: Relating to China or the Sinae; used in academic or historical contexts.
examples: Sinaic trade routes influenced ancient economies.
They studied Sinaic inscriptions.

senses.definition: A mineral or mineral group name; also a geological term sometimes used for iron-rich materials (less common).
examples: The specimen was identified as a sinaite mineral.
Geologists noted sinaite in the rock sample.
